item.title: This ABC Showdown Is Different
item.description: The network is now in a stronger position to defend itself against the FCC.
item.dc:creator: Will Gottsegen

item.title: Voters Can Be Disenfranchised Now
item.description: Just say it’s because they’re Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Adam Serwer

item.title: The Fight-Club Rule on Gerrymandering
item.description: Florida’s state constitution prohibits redrawing maps for political advantage.
item.dc:creator: Russell Berman

item.title: Donald Trump’s Disturbing Welcome for King Charles
item.description: At the White House, the president embraced the idea that the nation is an Anglo-Saxon one.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Chait

item.title: Photos: A Century of Sidecar Racing
item.description: Images from the past century of motorcycle racers and their sidecar passengers performing acrobatic maneuvers to stay balanced on the track, and their ever-evolving rigs
item.dc:creator: Alan Taylor

item.title: Rise of the Blood Populist
item.description: Most Americans fully reject political violence. It’s time to differentiate between those who tolerate it and everyone else.
item.dc:creator: Adrienne LaFrance

item.title: The King’s Admirer in Chief
item.description: Trump’s fondness for Charles at times appeared to tip over into envy.
item.dc:creator: Matt Viser

item.title: Who Is the Real Base of the Democratic Party?
item.description: Jamal Simmons on lessons from the 2024 election, how Democrats can win in 2028, and who the real base of the Democratic Party is. Plus: Why the White House Correspondents’ Dinner feels so out of touch, andThe Magician, by Colm Tóibín.
item.dc:creator: David Frum

item.title: Miranda Priestly Hangs Up Her Own Coat Now
item.description: The Devil Wears Prada 2finds the magazine industry in a much less glamorous place.
item.dc:creator: David Sims

item.title: Today’sAtlanticTrivia: Quiz Deliberately
item.description: Test your knowledge—and read our stories for a little extra help.
item.dc:creator: Drew Goins

item.title: The Pope Goes on an ICE Ride-Along
item.description: The Holy Father accepts Tom Homan’s gracious invitation.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Petri

item.title: The YOLO Presidency
item.description: Trump is focused on becoming one of history’s “great men.”
item.dc:creator: Ashley Parker

item.title: Sam Altman and Elon Musk Sure Dislike Each Other
item.description: The trial between the CEOsmakes the AI boom seem sordid and small.
item.dc:creator: Matteo Wong

item.title: The Evolution of Trump’s Corruption
item.description: The president is no longer intimidated by backlash.
item.dc:creator: David A. Graham

item.title: The Ballroom Truthers Have a Theory
item.description: The fake-assassination-attempt conspiracy keeps growing.
item.dc:creator: Kaitlyn Tiffany

item.title: A Ballroom Would Have Solved This
item.description: Many historical disasters would have been prevented if we had one of these in the White House.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Petri

item.title: Atlantic Reads:Screen PeopleWith Megan Garber
item.description: Staff writer Megan Garber and Adrienne LaFrance,The Atlantic’s executive editor, discuss Garber’s new book,Screen People: How We Entertained Ourselves Into a State of Emergency.

item.title: Calling Trump a Tyrant Is Not a Call to Violence
item.description: Conservatives want to police how we talk about Trump—while excusing how the president talks about everyone else.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Chait

item.title: Lee Friedlander’s America
item.description: The photographer spent decades meandering across the country with his camera. What was he looking for?
item.dc:creator: Hua Hsu

item.title: The Avant-Garde Path to God
item.description: A new book explores how contemporary art can offer glimpses of the divine.
item.dc:creator: Luis Parrales
